title: Creating Your First Business Document
description: Build a professional document from scratch using Microsoft Word, applying real workplace formatting standards including page setup, margins, and orientation.
objectives: Set page margins, orientation, and gutter margins
content:
Starting a Professional Document

Every workplace document starts with a purpose. Whether it's a letter, memo, or report, the first impression comes from how the document looks. In this lesson, you will create a business letter from scratch.

Step-by-Step

  1. Open Word and select a blank document.
  2. Set the font to a professional choice like Calibri, Arial, or Times New Roman, size 11 or 12.
  3. Set margins to Normal (1 inch on all sides) for most business documents. For documents that will be bound, use a gutter margin to add extra space on the side or top margin so text isn't obscured by the binding.
  4. Set orientation to Portrait for letters and reports, Landscape for wide tables or charts.
  5. Add a header with your company name and contact information. Use Insert > Header.
  6. Add page numbers using Insert > Page Number, usually at the bottom center or right.
  7. Type your content with clear paragraphs. Leave a blank line between paragraphs.
  8. Save as a .docx with a proper name. Then export as PDF for sending.

Page Setup and Margins

Page setup controls how your document fits on the page. Go to Layout > Margins to choose preset margins or create custom ones. A gutter margin is especially important for reports that will be printed and bound. Without it, text near the binding can be difficult to read.

Workplace Scenario

Your manager asks you to write a formal letter to a client confirming a meeting. You open Word, set the font to Calibri 11, add your company letterhead in the header, set 1-inch margins with a 0.5-inch gutter for binding, and type the letter with proper spacing. You save it as 2024-03-20_MeetingConfirmation_BrightTech.docx and export a PDF to email.

downloads.title: Business Letter Template
downloads.description: A clean Word template for formal letters

exercise: Create a formal business letter in Word. Address it to a fictional client, confirm a meeting for next Tuesday at 10:00 AM, and include your contact details. Add a header with a fictional company name and page numbers. Set margins to 1 inch on all sides with a 0.5-inch gutter margin. Set the page orientation to Portrait. Save as .docx and export as PDF.

quiz.question: Which font size is most common for professional business documents?
quiz.explanation: Size 11 or 12 is standard for readability and professionalism in business documents.

quiz.question: Where do you add page numbers in Word?
quiz.explanation: The Insert tab contains the Page Number option for adding automatic numbering.

quiz.question: What is a gutter margin used for?
options: To add color to the page
To add extra space for binding so text isn't obscured
quiz.explanation: A gutter margin adds extra space on the side or top of a page so that text is not hidden by the binding when printed.

quiz.question: When should you use Landscape orientation?
options: For wide tables or charts that do not fit in Portrait
quiz.explanation: Landscape orientation is useful for wide tables, charts, or documents that need more horizontal space.
